chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, our next problem is job sequencing with deadlines.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This problem we are going to solve it using greedy method.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So first of all we will understand what the problem is.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Already I have an example.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Through an example we will understand what the problem is.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then we will see how to solve it using greedy method.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us know about the problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See here in example five jobs are given.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Five tasks are given.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And if that task is completed we will get this profit.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So associated with each job there is some profit.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But that job must be finished within this deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It must be completed within the deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Like first job should be completed within a deadline 2 and deadline job 2 also 2.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Job 3 is 1.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So like that every job is having some deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the problem is jobs are given.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Every job is having some profit and if you complete the job you will get the profit and

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: you have to complete that job within a deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us understand what all these means.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Assume that there is a machine.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: On that each job has to be processed.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Each job takes one unit of time for completion.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the assumption.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the assumption in the problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is this one unit?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will assume this as 1 hour.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So on a machine each job needs 1 hour for its completion.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So you can take an example like a DTP operator or see mostly for making a legal documents

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on a bond paper.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We will find people in the market who will prepare documents.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose that preparation you take the job.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So he is a typist.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And he completes each job in 1 hour.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that example you can take.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now what is the situation?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us understand.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See assume that in the morning he has opened the shop.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The moment he has opened the shop these 5 people are standing there for getting their

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: jobs done.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So you have to take this situation to understand the problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And each person is having his own deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: He is saying that I want my job to be done in first hour only.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Can you do it or not?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you can do it, do it.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this person is saying that I am ready to wait for 3 hours.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No doubt my job needs only 1 hour but I am ready to wait for 3 hours.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose it is morning 9 o'clock.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So he is ready to wait till 12 o'clock.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And he is ready to wait till 11 o'clock.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And he is ready to wait till 10 o'clock.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So whose job should be done first?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This job should be done first.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But what is the profit we are gaining?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If this job is done, let us say this is rupees and we get just 10 rupees.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And if this job is done then we get 20 rupees.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So obviously which job should be done first?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Highest profit job should be done first.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the problem is we want the set of those jobs which can be completed within their deadlines

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: such that the profit is maximized.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is a maximization problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And what is the constraint in the problem?

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Job must be completed within the deadline.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is suitable for greedy method because it is an optimization problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It's a maximization problem.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we can apply greedy method.

chunks.text: Now let us solve this problem using greedy method.

chunks.text: Let us see how we can apply greedy method.

chunks.title: Job Sequencing with Deadlines - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So first of all how many hours are available for that typist to complete the job?

text: Hi, our next problem is job sequencing with deadlines. This problem we are going to solve it using greedy method. So first of all we will understand what the problem is. Already I have an example. Through an example we will understand what the problem is. Then we will see how to solve it using greedy method. So let us know about the problem. See here in example five jobs are given. Five tasks are given. And if that task is completed we will get this profit. So associated with each job there is some profit. But that job must be finished within this deadline. It must be completed within the deadline. Like first job should be completed within a deadline 2 and deadline job 2 also 2. Job 3 is 1. So like that every job is having some deadline. So the problem is jobs are given. Every job is having some profit and if you complete the job you will get the profit and you have to complete that job within a deadline. Let us understand what all these means. Assume that there is a machine. On that each job has to be processed. Each job takes one unit of time for completion. This is the assumption. This is the assumption in the problem. So what is this one unit? I will assume this as 1 hour. So on a machine each job needs 1 hour for its completion. So you can take an example like a DTP operator or see mostly for making a legal documents on a bond paper. We will find people in the market who will prepare documents. Suppose that preparation you take the job. So he is a typist. And he completes each job in 1 hour. That's it. So that example you can take. Now what is the situation? Let us understand. See assume that in the morning he has opened the shop. The moment he has opened the shop these 5 people are standing there for getting their jobs done. So you have to take this situation to understand the problem. And each person is having his own deadline. He is saying that I want my job to be done in first hour only. Can you do it or not? If you can do it, do it. And this person is saying that I am ready to wait for 3 hours. No doubt my job needs only 1 hour but I am ready to wait for 3 hours. Suppose it is morning 9 o'clock. So he is ready to wait till 12 o'clock. And he is ready to wait till 11 o'clock. And he is ready to wait till 10 o'clock. So whose job should be done first? This job should be done first. But what is the profit we are gaining? If this job is done, let us say this is rupees and we get just 10 rupees. And if this job is done then we get 20 rupees. So obviously which job should be done first? Highest profit job should be done first. Now the problem is we want the set of those jobs which can be completed within their deadlines such that the profit is maximized. So this is a maximization problem. And what is the constraint in the problem? Job must be completed within the deadline. So this is suitable for greedy method because it is an optimization problem. It's a maximization problem. So we can apply greedy method. Now let us solve this problem using greedy method. Let us see how we can apply greedy method. So first of all how many hours are available for that typist to complete the job? See one of the person or two people are ready to wait till 12 o'clock or for 3 hours they are ready to wait. So after that nobody is there. Suppose this is the only job that he has when he has opened the shop and he has to complete this within the deadline. How many slots he is having? From 0th hour to 1st hour, 1 unit of time. In this hour he can complete one job. And 1st hour to 2nd hour he can complete second job and 2nd to 3rd he can finish third job. So it means nobody is ready to wait beyond 3 units of time, 3 hours. So he has to finish jobs within these 3 slots only. If I give the time this is 9 o'clock to 10 o'clock and this is 10 o'clock to 11 o'clock, 11 to 12 o'clock. Now there are only 3 slots how many jobs he can do? So he can do only 3 jobs out of 5 he has to select just 3 so that the profit is maximum. Now if we apply greeding method then on what basis we will select the objects? We will select the objects in the order of their maximum profit. So first maximum profit then next maximum next maximum and so on. So let us see how to solve this one. Who is having highest profit? This one. So you can see already that the jobs are already arranged in the decreasing order of the profit. So I have to consider them in the same order. This is having highest profit. Now I will select this job for completion. I will select it I will not perform it. But in which slot I should do? He is ready to wait for 2 units of time means he came at 9 o'clock he is ready to wait till 11 o'clock and his job needs only 1 unit of time. So let us do it in this slot. Now let us pick up the next job. Second highest profit is 15 and what is his deadline? 2. Now again go to 1 to 2. He is ready to wait till 11 with 2 units of time but this is already occupied. Now see if there are any slots available on this side. Yes it is available. So that job too here. Now let us select the next job. Third job. Its deadline is 1. Can we do it? Let us see. 1 means what? 0 to 1 within this one. No this slot is already gone. So this job is rejected. We cannot do this job. So we have selected already these 2 jobs. Then what about this job? This can be done because the deadline is 3. So he is ready to wait till 12 o'clock or 3rd unit and every job needs 1 unit of time. Remember that. So we will do this in this slot. So that's it. And what about this job? 3rd already occupied. Is there any slot free on this side? No. If there is no slot available there you can look for the slots in this side. So there is no job slot available. That's all. We have a solution. What are the jobs that we are going to do? We are going to complete jobs 2 then job 1 and job 4 and in which sequence we have to do them. So here both the jobs are having deadline 2. So we can do them in sequence j1 then j2 then j4 or else j2 to j1 then j4. We can complete them in any of the sequences. So these are the sequences possible for completing those jobs as both are ready to wait for 2 hours. Total profit will be this job is 20 and this job is 15 and the profit of this job is 5. So it is 20 plus 15 plus 5 is 40. So the total profit that we gain will be 40. Now let us solve this. Here I have taken a table. J1 I will write. J2. Can we assign a slot to this second job like J1 was here? Yes J2 can come here. So we will assign a slot from 0 to 1 and already 1 to 2 was already assigned to this one. So already two slots are over you see. And we have the solution J1 and J2. How much profit we have? 20 plus the profit of the second job is 15. So this is 35 I am just keeping it as it is. Now next job to be considered J3 third job. Its deadline is 1. 0 to 1 already occupied. But every job needs just 1 unit of time. So how many slots we have with 1 1 unit? 4 slots we have. Now let us solve this by selecting the jobs. So the first job first job. What is the deadline? 3. Is the slot vacant? 2 to 3. Means not beyond 3. So 2 to 3. Yes job 1 can be done and we got the profit 35. I will write just below this one. Then next job J2. Deadline is 4. So check 3 to 4. Yes it's free. It can be done. So J2 and the profit that we got is 30. Then J3. J3. It's deadline is 4. No 3 to 4 is already occupied. Then check this side. If it can be done early. Yes already occupied. Here yes it can be done. See this means that this person is ready to wait for 4 units of time. So 4 hours. We can finish it early also.
